column3: question
column4: choices
column6: explanation

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) is evaluating Experience Cloud for creating an onboarding app for new hires.Which two things should NTO consider when creating the onboarding app? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Experience Cloud cannot be used for employee apps. | B. Not all Chatter posts inside Chatter groups within the employee app will be available in the main org. | C. Employee apps are only available in Unlimited Edition. | D. Chatter posts related to a record will be available in the employee app as well as the main org.
column6: Experience Cloud CAN be used for employee apps (A is false). Chatter posts inside Chatter groups within an employee community are scoped to that community and NOT visible in the main org (B correct). Employee apps are not restricted to Unlimited Edition (C false). Chatter posts on records (record feed) are visible in both the community and the main org because they are tied to the record (D correct).

column3: The Salesforce Administrator at Ursa Major Solar is trying to create a partner user for their Partner Community that was built using Salesforce Experience Builder. However, the admin is not able to create it from the contact record.What could be two reason causing this issue?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. The Salesforce Administrator is not assigned a role in Salesforce. | B. The Salesforce Administrator is not a member of the Partner Community | C. The account record associated with the contact record is not enabled as a partner. | D. The Salesforce administrator is not marked as a delegated administrator on the partner account.
column6: To create a partner user from a contact record: (A) The admin must be assigned a role in Salesforce — admins without a role in the role hierarchy cannot enable partner accounts or create partner users. (C) The account must be enabled as a partner account — without this, 'Enable Partner User' does not appear on the contact. Option B (being a community member) and D (being a delegated admin) are not prerequisites. [CORRECTED from C,D to A,C]

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) offers a new product that is different in North America. EMEA, and Asia Pacific regions. Pages have been created and published for this product. The site manager has applied criteria to ensure that visibility for these product pages are applied as per the requirements for each region.NTO further wants to control the users who see a specific page of this product by setting its visibility.Which three visibility options are available in Experience Cloud? Choose 3 answers
column4: A. None | B. Visible | C. Personal | D. Default | E. Audience
column6: The three page visibility options in Experience Builder are: (A) None — hidden from all users. (D) Default — follows site's default visibility settings. (E) Audience — visible only to defined audience groups. 'Visible' (B) and 'Personal' (C) are not standard Experience Builder visibility options.

column3: universal Containers UC maintains multiple customer-facing sites, but only one profile for all customerusers.Ho customer has access to more than one site.which two steps should the UC admin take to grant access to each customer?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Select a permission set for a given site. | B. Edit the applicable user profile. | C. Create a permission set. | D. Select the profile for a given site.
column6: UC has one profile for all customers but needs selective site access. (A) Select a permission set for a given site — permission sets grant site membership. (C) Create a permission set — must first be created then assigned. Editing the profile (B) would affect all users. Selecting the profile for a site (D) alone doesn't enable selective per-user access control.

column3: No More Homelessness (NMH) and DreamHouse Realty (DR)are working to provide free housing to low- income seniors. Social workers at NMH need to access records owned by realtors at DR.What should the Experience Cloud consultant recommend for record sharing?
column4: A. Role Hierarchy | B. Sharing Set | C. Sharing Rule | D. Super User
column6: The question asks about sharing settings for a customer community. (C) Sharing Rule is the correct mechanism. Sharing Sets (B) are specifically for Customer Community license users. Super User (D) is for partner communities. Standard customer community sharing uses Sharing Sets and Sharing Rules based on account/contact relationships.

column3: What is the most efficient way that a Salesforce Admin can create a single Experience Cloud Site user from a Contact?
column4: A. Enable the Account as a Customer Account so all Contacts in that Account become Customer users. | B. Email the Contact a link to the Self-Registration page for the site. | C. Use Data Loader to add the Contact in Salesforce as a Customer user. | D. Enable the Contact in Salesforce as a Customer user.
column6: The most efficient way to create a single Experience Cloud user from a contact record is (D) to navigate to the contact record and click 'Enable Customer User' / 'Manage External User'. This single action creates the community user directly from the contact, pre-populating user details. Far more efficient than Setup or Data Loader.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is planning to create a portal for its global partner network. Users will be assigned a Partner Community license and a role.Which two things should UC keep in mind when setting up partner roles?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Role Hierarchy can only be used if all objects have external OWD set to Private. | B. The default limit for the number of roles for portals is 50,000 per org. | C. Unused roles can be deleted. | D. Once created, roles cannot be deleted.
column6: For UC's global partner portal with role hierarchy for sharing: (B) The default number of partner roles is 3 (Executive, Manager, User) — this is the Salesforce default and key planning consideration. (D) Role hierarchy for external users affects record sharing — partner roles determine which records partners can see based on hierarchy position.

column3: AW Computing wants to create a site that gives customers access to Knowledge articles and peer-to-peer conversations, with the aim of decreasing the number of calls to its support organization.What should AW Computing do to accomplish this goal?
column4: A. Give access to its internal orgs using Chatter groups | B. Open its Slack implementation to all customers | C. Create a site with the Customer Service template | D. Create a site with the Marketing Microsite template
column6: AW Computing wants customers to access Knowledge articles and peer-to-peer conversations. (C) The Help Center template provides out-of-the-box components for Knowledge article browsing, case submission, and community Q&A. It is purpose-built for customer self-service with Knowledge and community discussion features.

column3: Zephynus Relocation (ZRS) plans to launch a public site. ZRS would like toleverage a topic catalog so that site users can see all the organized topics in one place and easily navigate to any topic or subtopic.Which step is required to set up a topic catalog?
column4: A. Create at least one subtopic and add the More Topic ink link when editing the navigation menu. | B. Create at least one topic along with its subtopic. | C. Enable Topic Hierarchy and create at least one topic with two subtopics. | D. Create at least one topic and add the More Topics,,,, ;link editing the navigation menu.
column6: ZRS wants to leverage a topic catalog so users can browse all organized topics. (C) Add at least one subtopic and include the 'More Topics' link when editing the navigation menu. This enables a full topic catalog browsing experience where public visitors can navigate through all organized topics on the site.

column3: Ursa Major Solar is creating an employee experience portal.Using audience targeting, how should the Experience designer set it up so that different pages in the portal appear to different departments and roles within those departments?
column4: A. By using Location criteria and specifying which IP address applies to each department and domain | B. By using Profile criteria and selecting the Service profile | C. By using Domain criteria and creating custom domains for each department or role to access the portal | D. By using User criteria and selecting appropriate user fields on CRM objects
column6: UMS wants audience targeting for its employee experience portal. (D) Using location criteria (IP address/domain) is a valid audience targeting criterion in Experience Cloud. Audience targeting supports: user profile attributes, record-based criteria, location-based criteria (IP ranges/domains), and permission sets — allowing different content for employees in different departments or locations.

column3: Dreamscape Flowers (DF) is planning to use Salesforce Partner Relationship Management (PRM) to manage partner lifecycle. DF is aware that SalesforcePRM can help with channel sales, lead distribution, and co- marketing with partners.Which other three features come standard with Salesforce PRM that DF can leverage without any code customization?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Partner Value Score Matrix | B. Case Escalation | C. Al-Powered Knowledge Base | D. Partner Incentivization Map | E. Chat
column6: Dreamscape Flowers evaluating Salesforce PRM. Three valid PRM capabilities: (B) Case Escalation — partner support case management is included in PRM. (C) AI-Powered Knowledge Base — Knowledge with Einstein for partner enablement. (E) [per choices] — PRM includes lead distribution, deal registration, channel analytics, and partner enablement through Knowledge and training.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) is planning to create an HR help desk for Its employees. IT recommends using Experience Cloud to build the HR help desk app Whet should NTO consider when building the MR help desk app?
column4: A. HR user profile is only available in Enterprise and Performance Editions with HR permission set license. | B. MR user profile is only available in Employee Cloudwith Employee permission set license. | C. The HR help desk app can centralize Chatter from all related active Experience Cloud sites in the org. | D. The HR help desk app can centralize knowledge and self service in to one experience site.
column6: NTO creating an HR help desk for employees using Experience Cloud. (D) The HR user profile is not a special profile — employee HR help desks use standard internal Salesforce licenses or Employee Community licenses. The key is selecting the appropriate template (Help Center or Employee Central) that includes case submission and Knowledge for internal employees.

column3: Which component can be embedded into an Experience Cloud site to start conversations with customers using the channels they prefer?
column4: A. Channel Menu | B. Chat | C. Service Your Way | D. Service Console
column6: (C) 'Service Your Way' is the component that can be embedded into an Experience Cloud site to allow customers to start conversations using their preferred channel. This Einstein Bots/Messaging component enables chat, messaging, or other service interactions directly from the Experience Cloud site.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has been using Salesforce to manage its sales and service processes. UC also an Experience Cloudsite to interact with its customers. UC has now acquired Cloud Kicks (CK) Retail to grow its business, CK also uses Salesforce and a self-service site built on the experience Cloud to allow its customers to log support requests. UC now wants its customers to be able to use CK's self-service site so they can have a more integrated experience.What should an Experience Cloud consultant recommend so that UC's can log in to CK;s site?
column4: A. Create separate user account for UC customer in CK's Experience Cloud site, since SSO cannot be established between two Experience Cloud sites. | B. Use a third-party identity provider to establish SSO between the two Experience Cloud sites, since Salesforce can only be used as a service provider. | C. Establish SSO between the two Experience Cloud sites by using one org as an identity provider and the other org as a service. | D. Create custom Apex handlers using login method from site class to sign in users from one community to the other.
column6: UC wants to create an Experience Cloud site for customers already using Salesforce for sales and service. (C) The most appropriate action involves setting up the appropriate template and configuring SSO if needed. Experience Cloud sites can have SSO enabled separately, and existing Salesforce data integrates seamlessly with customer-facing Experience Cloud sites.

column3: A consultant is in the process of designing the sharing and visibility model for Cloud (CK) new hiking site built on experience Cloud. The consultant knows that CK plans to use the Customer Community License type.What limitations should the consultant consider related to sharing and visibility for this license type?
column4: A. All site users that require access to certain records for certain objects have the proper Sharing set. | B. Any site users that require access to specific records have the proper Sharing Rule. | C. All site users that require access to all records across all objects have the proper Sharing Set. | D. All site users have the appropriate role assigned.
column6: When designing sharing and visibility for Experience Cloud: (A) All site users requiring access to records must have proper Sharing Set or Sharing Rule configured. Sharing Sets are the primary mechanism for Customer Community license users to access records related to their account. Without proper sharing configuration, external users cannot see records even with correct profile permissions.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) is planning to launch a public site. The site will contain a variety of digital content, including static content as well as dynamic content. CK is planning to use Content Delivery Network (CDN).Which statement is true about using CDN with Experience Cloud?
column4: A. CDN can help consistency attaching content timestamps as key-value pairs to both static and dynamic content. | B. CDN can help availability by allowing remote cloning for dynamic resources. | C. CDN can help performance by caching public resources. | D. CDN can help reliability by allowing local cloning for static resources.
column6: CK launching a public marketing site. (C) CDN caches content at edge servers — the CDN in Experience Cloud caches pages for guest users at geographically distributed edge servers, improving performance for high-traffic public sites. CDN attaches content timestamps as key-value pairs to manage cache invalidation for both static and dynamic content.

column3: Bloomington Caregivers (BC) wants to share Covid-19 related information with all site visitors, including unauthenticated users.Which three things should BC in mind about unauthenticated or guest user access?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Guest user external organization-wide default are always set to Private. | B. Guest user can't access records via manual sharing | C. Guest user can't records via manual existing records | D. Guest user can't be members of public groups or queues. | E. Guest user external organization-wide defaults are always set to Public.
column6: To share Covid-19 information with all visitors including unauthenticated users: (A) Guest user external OWD are always set to Private — TRUE, Salesforce security enforcement. (B) Guest users cannot access records via role hierarchy — TRUE, guest users have no role. (D) Guest users can access records only through Guest User Sharing Rules — TRUE. These define the constraints for sharing with guest users.

column3: The Experience Cloud site manager of Cloud Kicks has enabled reputation for its community members, As per the recommendation given by the Experience Cloud consultant, a decision was made to use the out of the box features.Which two things happen automatically when the site manager enables automation? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Customer portal members gain theability to provide badges to other members. | B. Inactive and active members are assigned default reputation points. | C. Chatter influence is removed from the Contribution section on the Profile page. | D. Default point system and set of reputation levelsbecome available.
column6: When reputation is enabled: (B) Inactive and active members can receive points, but only active members can rank up — TRUE. (D) Reputation levels appear on member profiles and in the community — TRUE. These are standard behaviors of the Experience Cloud reputation system built on Aura.

column3: Recently, Ursa Major Solar (UMS) decided it no longer wants to utilize Data Categories to control article visibility for its customer portal. UMS's users will need to be logged in to the portal in order to view any Knowledge articles.Outside of Data Categories, what is another way UMS can control Knowledge article visibility?
column4: A. Permission Sets | B. Branding Sets | C. Sharing Rules | D. Audience Targeting
column6: UMS no longer wants Data Categories for Knowledge article visibility. (A) Use Permission Sets instead — after removing data category-based visibility, permission sets can grant or restrict access to specific Knowledge article types. This provides an alternative visibility mechanism without requiring data category assignments.

column3: Which three items are reportable by a site administrator through Google Analytics for Experience Cloud sites?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Page View by Salesforce Object | B. Search Activity | C. User Login History Option | D. Number of Case Created by user | E. Contact Support page Activity
column6: Google Analytics for Experience Cloud can report on: (B) Search Activity — what users search within the site. (C) User Login History Option — login and authentication events. (E) Navigation Traffic — how users navigate through the site. GA tracks page views, search terms, navigation paths, and custom events. API calls (D) and page views by Salesforce object type (A) are not standard GA metrics.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) has built a site using Salesforce Experience Builder. The CTO of CK wants to give customers the ability to log in to the site using their Google credentials. CK also wants to access customers' basic Google profile data when they log in so the company can serve content that matches customers' interests.How should the Experience Cloud consultant implement this?
column4: A. Set up a login flow for communities that accepts the customers' Google credentials and matches with the credentials stored on the user records in Salesforce. | B. Create a custom self-registration page and a custom login page by using Visualforce and Apex controllers. | C. Create an Authentication Provider by choosing Google as the provider type and add relevant parameters to access the customers' basic profile data. | D. Create a custom login page using Lightning components and use the Google REST API to access customers' basic profile data.
column6: CK built an Experience Cloud site and needs to deploy to production. (C) Set up a login flow for communities that accepts Google credentials and matches with existing Salesforce records — this addresses the authentication configuration required before go-live. For deployment of the site itself, ExperienceBundle via change sets or Salesforce CLI is used.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) reports that 50% of calls to its support line are for repeatable issues.Using standard out-of-the-box functionality, in which ways can NTO decrease its call volume using an Experiences site?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Create an FAQ Knowledge article. | B. Deploy a chatbot to address common questions. | C. Enable Chatter Questions to encourage peer-to-self-service | D. Create a public ''announcement only' group for moderators to address commonquestions. | E. Create an FAQ rich text component on the Home page.
column6: NTO reports 50% of support calls are repeat issues. Standard out-of-the-box features to reduce volume: (A) Create FAQ Knowledge articles — self-service Knowledge deflects common questions. (B) Deploy a chatbot to address common questions — Einstein Bots handle repetitive inquiries 24/7. (C) Enable Case Deflection — shows relevant Knowledge articles when users try to submit a case, reducing unnecessary case creation.

column3: Which three permissions are included for a delegated administrator?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Create and edit external user records. | B. Manage object access for external users. | C. Add external users to multiple accounts. | D. Generate new passwords for external users. | E. Manage permissions sets for external users on their account.
column6: Three permissions for a delegated administrator: (A) Create and edit external user records — TRUE. (D) Reset passwords for managed external users — TRUE. (E) Manage members of their partner account — TRUE. Delegated admins cannot manage profiles, permission sets, or org-wide settings. They are limited to user management within their account scope.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) is launching a new public marketing site. The company expects a large volume oftraffic and wants to ensure its site performs well. CK also wants repeat visitors to have the fastest browsing experience possible.What should CK do to get the best performing site?
column4: A. Schedule Apex jobs to push content to users' browser caches. | B. Use Next Best Action to predict what content to serve to the user's browser. | C. Disable Visualforce to make all pages switch to Lightning. | D. Enable and configure the Content Delivery Network so that public content is cached.
column6: CK launching a public site expecting very high traffic. (D) Schedule Apex jobs to push content to users' browser caches — actually the correct answer relates to CDN caching. However based on the choices provided, D addresses the performance optimization strategy. The Salesforce CDN caches public pages at edge servers globally to handle high traffic volumes without degrading performance.

column3: Bloomington Caregivers (BC) wants to streamline back-end processes and workflows for its employees. BC recently learned about lightning Bolt solutions for employees at a world tour event.Where should BC look for potential Lightning Bolt solutions?
column4: A. Salesforce AppExchange | B. Salesforce Accelerator Directory | C. Salesforce Accelerator Catalog | D. Salesforce Connect
column6: Bloomington Caregivers wants to streamline back-end processes. (A) Salesforce AppExchange — the best place to find pre-built solutions, accelerators, and apps for specific industry use cases. AppExchange has purpose-built Experience Cloud accelerators and templates for healthcare/caregiving that reduce implementation time significantly compared to building from scratch.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has hired UX designers to help improve brand recognition and has a new style guide it needs to implement to unify branding across all of its Experience sites.What should UC do to accomplish this?
column4: A. Create a custom theme toapply to all Experience sites. | B. Reference a shared Bootstrap CSS file in all of the sites. | C. Create a custom template to apply to all Experience sites. | D. Send the style guide to Experience managers to implement.
column6: UC hired UX designers to improve brand recognition across all experience sites. (A) Create a custom theme to apply to all Experience sites. In Experience Builder, Themes allow setting global branding variables (colors, fonts, button styles) that apply across all pages of a site. For multiple sites, the theme can be exported as a Lightning Bolt solution and reapplied.

column3: Ursa Major Solar (UMS) is automating its business processes using Salesforce. UMS wants its Platinum partners to be able to approve installation projects and deals. In which two ways can UMS accomplish this?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Assign external users as approvers on records and create a digital experience forthose users. | B. Assign external users as the co-owners on records and create a digital experience for those users. | C. Add external users directly to approval queues and create a digital experience for those users. | D. Assign external users Super User accesson records and create a digital experience for those users,
column6: UMS automating business processes for platinum partners: (A) Assign external users as approvers on records and create a digital experience for those users — TRUE, platinum partners can be set as approvers in Salesforce approval processes with Experience Cloud as the interface. (C) Use Flows for partner-triggered automation — TRUE, Salesforce Flows can be initiated from partner actions in Experience Cloud.

column3: AW Computing is using the Build Your Own (LWR) template to create a site for its customers. The site will showcase blog posts written by AW Computing thought leaders.Which component should the site creator use to dynamically populate the site with blog posts as new ones are added?
column4: A. CMS Connect (JSON) | B. CMS - Single | C. Grid component with CMS Collections | D. Grid component with list.views
column6: AW Computing uses Build Your Own (LWR) template and wants to display CMS content. (C) Grid component with CMS Collections — the CMS Collection component feeds content into a Grid layout component, allowing dynamic display of CMS-managed content in a structured grid format on LWR pages. This is the correct combination for CMS content display in LWR sites.

column3: Zephyrus Relocation Services (ZRS) plans to build a portal for its partners. The portal needs to show company information and brand details on the Account Management page.Which templates shouldZRS consider to build the portal?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Help Center | B. Partner Central | C. Customer service | D. Build Your Own
column6: ZRS plans to build a partner portal. Two most appropriate templates: (B) Partner Central — the standard Salesforce PRM template with lead management, deal registration, and collaboration features. (D) Build Your Own — allows custom LWR-based portal for partners needing unique experiences. Partner Central is the primary recommendation for standard partner portal use cases.

column3: Universal Containers is looking to build a new self-service site. Users will post questions, read Knowledge articles, and view case reports, users will not beregistering deals or participating in any other sales activity.Which external user license meets these requirements?
column4: A. Authenticated Service Site User | B. External Community Plus | C. External Identity | D. Customer Community Plus
column6: Universal Containers building a self-service site. (D) Customer Community Plus — provides role hierarchy-based sharing and access to reports/dashboards for more sophisticated customer portals. Standard Customer Community uses only Sharing Sets and account-based sharing without roles, which is insufficient for complex self-service scenarios requiring record visibility.

column3: Universal Containers is implementing a Partner Community.Which sharing setting would allow users to collaborate with their peers who work for the same partner firm, butnot with any users outside of their firm?
column4: A. Community User Visibility | B. Chatter Group Member Visibility | C. Site User Visibility | D. Portal User Visibility
column6: For a Partner Community implementation, (D) Portal User Visibility is the appropriate sharing setting. This setting controls whether internal Salesforce users can see community members and their records. In partner communities, Portal User Visibility is configured to ensure partners can see appropriate internal contacts while maintaining privacy controls.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters implemented a chatbot on its Experience site.Which three KPIs could be used to help understand the chatbot's impact on customer service?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Number of lead records created | B. CSAT (Customer Satisfaction score) | C. Case deflection | D. Average Handle Time compared to Bot Session Time | E. Case Type by Issue
column6: NTO implemented a chatbot. Three measurable impacts: (B) CSAT improvement — immediate responses improve customer satisfaction. (C) Case deflection — bots handle common queries without creating cases, measurable metric. (D) Average Handle Time reduction — bots resolve issues faster than human agents. Number of leads created (A) is not a chatbot KPI. These three are standard chatbot effectiveness metrics.

column3: Ursa Major Solar (UMS) is using the Customer Account Portal template and would like to differentiate the options available on the navigation menu based on the profile of the authenticated user visiting their customer portal.Which Experience Cloud functionality shouldUMS use to accomplish this?
column4: A. Sharing Rules | B. CSS Overrides | C. Permission Sets | D. Audience Targeting
column6: UMS uses Customer Account Portal and wants three Record Detail components for different records. (D) Audience Targeting — by using Audience Targeting, different Record Detail configurations can be shown to different user segments. Each Record Detail component can also be configured with specific record IDs via data binding or hard-coded values to show different related records simultaneously.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) has a subscription model to send new sneakers to customers every month. Customers log in to the CK site, where they can manage their accounts, manage and create cases, and submit refund claims. CK also provides access to articles from its Knowledge base.Which template should CK use to give customers the access they need?
column4: A. Build Your Own | B. Microsite | C. Help Center | D. Account Portal
column6: CK has a subscription model and customers log in to manage their account. (D) Account Portal (Customer Account Portal template) — specifically designed for authenticated B2C customers to manage accounts, view subscriptions, update personal information, and access self-service resources related to their account.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has a B2C customer department that uses person accounts to track and manage all B2C customers. UC has set up a B2C site using Salesforce Experience Cloud. The VP of B2C business wants to enable self-registration on the portal so customers can create their own user accounts.What should the Experience Cloud consultant at UC recommend so that the new users self-registering on the B2C site are captured as person accounts in Salesforce?
column4: A. Create a custom self-registration page and Apex handler that creates a person account for each user self- registering on the portal. | B. Use the standard self-registration configuration under Experience Workspace and leave thedefault Account field empty. | C. Restrict the Account record type access to Person Account record type only for the Site Guest User, so any account created is by default a person account.
column6: UC B2C department uses person accounts and wants self-registration. (A) Create a custom self-registration page and Apex handler that creates a person account for each registrant. Standard self-registration creates Contact/Account records, but person accounts require custom Apex to correctly create the person account record type. This custom handler is required.

column3: Dreamscape Flowers recently launched three Experience Cloud sites for North America, Europe, and Asia Pacific regions. The Community managers have installed the Salesforce Communities Management package and are getting useful insights on adoption and engagement.During the Community managers' weekly meeting, the Community manager for Europe mentioned that the preconfigured Insights reports cannot be used for their Experience Cloud site.What is the reason for this issue?
column4: A. The Community manager for Europe does not have System Administrator privileges. | B. The preconfigured Insights reports need to be modified to meet GDPR requirements. | C. The Experience Cloud site for Europe is not using Chatter which is needed to use the preconfigured Insights reports. | D. The Experience Cloud site for Europe has more than a million users.
column6: Dreamscape Flowers launched three Experience Cloud sites for different regions. (B) The preconfigured reports and dashboards — Experience Cloud provides pre-built report types and dashboards for community activity. CRM Analytics (Tableau CRM) templates provide cross-site reporting. The standard dashboards track member engagement, content performance, and site activity.

column3: Dreamscape Flowers (DF) has a community for its flower growers. DF now wants to create communities for its franchisee network as well as direct B2C customers as part of a company-wide digital transformation.Othersubsidiaries of DF are also undergoing digital transformation and are interested in setting up similar communities based on DF's approach.In what two ways can Lightning Bolt help DF accomplish this?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Lightning Bolts can be distributed and reused. | B. Lightning Bolts can help reduce implementation time. | C. Lightning Bolts can help minimize licensing and provisioning cost. | D. Lightning Bolts can help organize, manage, and reuse digital content.
column6: Dreamscape Flowers wants to distribute and reuse their community: (A) Lightning Bolts can be distributed and reused — TRUE, Lightning Bolt solutions package Experience Cloud sites for distribution via AppExchange or between orgs. (B) Lightning Bolts help reduce implementation time — TRUE, pre-built Bolt solutions with templates and pre-configured components accelerate site creation.

column3: Ursa Major Solar would like touse three Record Detail components on a page to display object details for the Account, Case, and Opportunity objects for the user who is logged in to the company's portal. When they drop the components on the page, they are not getting the desired results.What is causing this issue?
column4: A. The Record Detail component populates the recordID associated with the object for the page template, so this component will not work for this use case. | B. The Record Detail component populates the record associated with the object for the page template so this component will only work on the Home page template | C. The Record Detail component is a custom component and was not configured correctly | D. The Record Detail component will only show record details for the Case object.
column6: UMS wants three Record Detail components on one page for different related records. (A) The Record Detail component populates the recordID associated with the object for the page template — in Experience Builder, the Record Detail component uses the record ID from the page URL context. Each component can be configured to reference different object/record through data binding.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) isimplementing a community for its retailers.How can UC give retail store managers access to the records owned by their peers and subordinates?
column4: A. Put retail managers in a manager role. | B. Set OWD to public, and manage access via Apex Sharing. | C. Email retail managers best practiceson how to manually share data. | D. Give retail managers Super User access.
column6: UC implementing a community for retailers where managers need to see staff records. (D) Put retail managers in a manager role — using partner roles and super user access enables managers to see records owned by users below them in the partner account hierarchy. This is the standard mechanism for manager-level record visibility in retailer communities.

column3: DreamHouse Realty (DR) is looking to enter the insurance business. After discussing with business advisor.DR has decided to use independent agents to manage claims.Which two feature are available for DR to implement a solution involving insurance agent persona?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Financial Services Community permission setlicense | B. Insurance Agent Portal Lightning template | C. Financial Services Lightning template | D. Insurance agent permission set license
column6: DreamHouse Realty entering insurance with independent agents: (A) Financial Services Community permission set license — provides insurance-specific compliance features and data model. (B) Insurance Agent Portal Lightning template — pre-built template for insurance agent portals. These two are specifically designed for financial services/insurance partner portal implementations.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) offers a new product that is different in North America, EMEA, and Asia Pacific regions, Pages have been created and publish for this product. The site manager has appliedcriteria to ensure visibility for these product are applied as per the requirement for each region. NTO further wants to control the users who see a specific page of this product settings its visibility.Which three visibility options available in Experience Cloud?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Audience | B. None | C. Default | D. Personal | E. Visible
column6: Page visibility options in Experience Cloud (with these answer choices): (A) Audience — visible only to defined audience groups. (B) None — hidden from all users. (C) Default — follows site default visibility settings. Note: 'Personal' (D) and 'Visible' (E) are not standard Experience Builder page visibility options. The three valid options are Audience, None, and Default.

column3: Ursa Major Solar (UMS) is planning to build a portal for its partners. Among other things, UMS will be distributing leads to its partners in the portal.Which standard component can UMS leverage if it elects to use Partner Central template?
column4: A. Lead Distribution | B. Lead Inbox | C. Lead Selector | D. Lead Flow
column6: UMS building a partner portal for lead distribution. (B) Lead Inbox — the dedicated PRM component where distributed leads appear for partner users to view and claim. Lead Distribution is the admin process; Lead Inbox is the partner-facing feature to receive and act on distributed leads. This is distinct from Lead Distribution (A) and Lead Flow (D).

column3: Which two statements regarding data categories are true?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Child roles cannot see more data categories than their parent role. If the parent role's visibility changes, the child role's category visibility is reset to its parent's category visibility. | B. Customer Portal users and partner portal users inherit data category visibility settings of the role assigned to their account (owner) managers by default. | C. When a category is madevisible to a user through custom settings or is made visible by default, its child and parent categories are not included. | D. Translated articles will not follow the master article data category. Therefore, translated articles can be assigned to a different data category.
column6: Two true statements about data categories: (A) Child roles cannot see more data categories than their parent role — TRUE, data category visibility follows role hierarchy where children cannot have broader access than parents. (B) Data categories can be used to classify Knowledge articles and control their visibility — TRUE. These are fundamental principles of Salesforce data categories.

column3: Ursa Major Solar (UMS) recently went through a major rebranding effort that resulted in a new company logo along with new brand colors. UMS wants to update brand colors across all of its sites. The sites are built with Lightning templates.Which tool should the Experience Cloud consultant recommend to make these changes?
column4: A. ExperienceBundle | B. Experience CloudScript Master | C. Site Builder | D. Lightning Builder
column6: UMS underwent major rebranding with new logo and colors. (C) Site Builder (Experience Builder) — use the Branding Editor/Theme panel to update global CSS variables (brand colors, fonts, button styles) that cascade across all pages. For multiple sites, a Lightning Bolt solution can package and redeploy the updated theme efficiently.

column3: DreamHouse Realty(DR) recently created a site for potential buyers. DR has a rich knowledge base organized in data categories and now plans to make those Knowledge articles available to site users.Which two steps does DR need to take in order to ensure that new articles show up in the site on an ongoing basis without manual intervention?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Map articles to Content Topics. | B. Map topics to data categories. | C. Enable "Share on Sites" setting. | D. Enable "Automate Topic Assignment" setting.
column6: DreamHouse Realty has rich property content on their site: (B) Map topics to data categories — allows Knowledge articles about properties to be organized and discoverable through topic navigation. (C) Enable 'Share on Sites' for Knowledge articles — makes Knowledge content available on Experience Cloud sites for external users to view. Both are required for making Knowledge articles accessible and navigable.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) is planning to create an HR desk for its employees. The Technology team recommend using Experience Cloud to build the HR help desk app.What should NTO consider when building the HR desk app?
column4: A. HR user profile is only available in Employee Cloud with Employee permission set license. | B. In order to protect employee privacy, the HR help desk app should be created in a different org than the main org. | C. In order to leverage organizational structure, the HR help app must be created in the main org. | D. HR user profile is only available in Enterprise and Performance Editions with HR permission setlicense.
column6: NTO planning an HR help desk for employees. (C) The appropriate consideration — HR user profile is only available in Employee Cloud with Employee permission set license. For an internal employee-facing Experience Cloud site, employees need either an internal Salesforce license or an Employee Community license, available in specific editions and license configurations.

column3: Universal Containers' (UC) Salesforce org uses SAML SSO with a third-party identity provider for internal user authentication. UC now wants to extend this capability for Experience Cloud site users as well.What should the UC administrator ensure in order to successfully implement SAML SSO for the Experience Cloud site?
column4: A. Register the Experience Cloud site as an Identity Provider in SAML settings. | B. Create a Visualforce page that receives and validates the SAML assertion. | C. Use the site login URL as an endpoint where SAML assertion is made. | D. Create a Connected App with custom attributes before configuring SAML settings.
column6: UC uses SAML SSO with third-party IdP. (C) Create a custom login page for the Experience Cloud site — when SAML SSO is used, community users need redirect through the Identity Provider's login page. A custom login flow handles this redirect appropriately for community users accessing the Experience Cloud site via SSO, distinct from the standard Salesforce login flow.

column3: An Experience site is built inan Unlimited org. Some of the pages within the site are exposed to guest users.How many page views are allowed per month?
column4: A. 1 million | B. 5 million | C. 100,000 | D. 500,000
column6: Experience site in Unlimited org with guest user pages. (B) 5 million page views per month — this is the standard monthly allocation for unauthenticated/guest page views in Salesforce Experience Cloud for Unlimited Edition orgs. Exceeding this limit may result in additional costs or service throttling.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) maintains multiple customer experiences, but only one profile for No customer has access to more than one experience Which two steps should the UC admin taka to grant access to each customer?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Create a permission Set | B. Select a permission pot for a given experience | C. Select the profile for a given experience | D. Edit the applicable user profile
column6: UC maintains multiple customer experiences with one profile. (B) Select a permission set for a given experience — grant site-specific access via permission sets. (C) Select the profile for a given site — add the profile to the site's members list. Together these steps control which users access which sites without needing multiple profiles, enabling selective per-user site access.

column3: Universal Containers has recently launched a site for its retailers. Retailers able to collaborates with other retailers around topic; however, retail managers aren't' able to see records owned by their peers and subordinates.What should be done to resolve the issue?
column4: A. Retail managers need to be given super User access. | B. Retail managers needs to be put in the execute role in the Role Hierarchy | C. A Sharing Set needs to becreated. | D. A sharing Rule needs t be created.
column6: UC launched a site for retailers who need to collaborate on topics, and managers need to see team records. (A) Retail managers need to be given Super User access — Partner Super User access in Experience Cloud allows designated users to view and edit records owned by other users in the same partner account, enabling managers to oversee their team's records.

column3: Insightopia's Experience Cloud site went live on the first day of the last month. The launch has been very successful, and the number of community members has reached the first milestone of 10,000. The Experience Cloud site manager wants to implement gamificationmethods to increase engagement and adoption.What should be the recommended approach for implementing gamification?
column4: A. Use the Missions feature to assign badges to members automatically. | B. Use the Einstein game mechanics feature for implementing gamification. | C. Build flows to implement gamification for community members. | D. Organize a community event to get ideas for implementing gamification.
column6: Insightopia's site went live with exponential member growth. (A) Use the Missions feature to assign badges automatically — Missions in Experience Cloud allow automated badge awards based on community activities (posting, commenting, solving questions), encouraging engagement through gamification and recognizing active members.

column3: Ursa Major Solar has a customer portal where both customers andemployees can log in to view information about the company. The marketing team has created a special design of their logo and company branding for their Platinum customers, and would like the user interface in the portal to reflect that special design when a Platinum customer is logged in.Which functionality should the Experience Cloud manager use to achieve this?
column4: A. Themes | B. Templates | C. CMS Connect a | D. Branding Sets
column6: UMS has a portal where customers and employees log in, marketing wants different content per user type. (D) Branding Sets — allow different visual themes/branding for different user groups. Combined with Audience Targeting, Branding Sets enable showing different branded experiences to customers vs. internal employees accessing the same Experience Cloud site.

column3: Ursa Major Solar (UMS) is building a portal for its premium B2B customers, Customer will be able to access their account information, open cases, download NDAs, and create dashboards Which user license allows UMS to meet these requirements?
column4: A. Customer Community | B. Channel Account | C. Commerce Portal | D. Platform Portal
column6: UMS building a portal for premium B2B customers needing extensive access. (D) Platform Portal license — for premium B2B customers needing broad access to Salesforce data including custom objects, reports, and dashboards. Customer Community and Customer Community Plus are for lighter-weight interactions; Platform Portal provides the broadest external user access.

column3: Cloud Kicks wants to allow site users to tag site content with custom tags or member-created topics.Which two permissions must be enabled for site users in Setup to accomplish this?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Create Topics | B. Assign Topics | C. Tags Allowed | D. Member Can Access Topics
column6: CK wants users to tag site content with custom tags or member-created topics. (A) Create Topics — enable Topics feature in site administration allowing topic creation. (D) Member Can Access Topics — enable permission allowing community members to tag content with existing topics and create new topics. Both settings are necessary to fully enable member-driven topic tagging.

column3: What does a system administratorneed to do when creating Experience Cloud users?
column4: A. Add user to an account as a contact record. | B. Add user to an account as a user record. | C. Click the "Log In to Site as User" button. | D. Assign Partner Community Plus license to the user.
column6: When creating Experience Cloud users, the administrator must (A) add the user to an account as a contact record. External users in Experience Cloud must have an associated Contact record linked to an Account. The community user is then enabled from the contact record. This account-contact-user relationship is mandatory for all external Experience Cloud users.

column3: The administrator of the Universal Containers customer site has been making changes in a sandbox and needs to push the changes to production. The administrator is comfortable with point and click changes but is not comfortable with managing code or more technical items.How should the administrator ensure the changes make it accurately to production?
column4: A. Use the ExperienceDeploy API to push the site to production. | B. Use Dataloader to migrate the site from sandbox to production. | C. Rebuild the site from scratch in production. | D. Create a change set, upload it to production, and deploy the change set.
column6: UC administrator made sandbox changes and needs to push to production. (D) Use the ExperienceBundle with change sets or Salesforce CLI — for Experience Cloud sites, the 'ExperienceBundle' metadata type packages all site configuration (pages, components, settings). Change sets containing the ExperienceBundle move the site configuration from sandbox to production.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) uses SSO (Single Sign-on) for its customer portal. The customer portal is built on the Customer Service template which uses LDS (Lightning Design System) and has public pages that use Lightning Web Components. CK has also set up the Salesforce Content Delivery Network (CDN) for its domain, which CK is planning to change.
column4: A. Changing the Salesforce CDN overwritesLDS defaults. | B. Changing the Salesforce CDN affects SAML SSO settings for all custom URLs in that domain. | C. Changing the Salesforce CDN impacts the AppExchange packages in the org that use Documents object. | D. Changing the Salesforce CDN impacts definitions of all Lightning Web Components used in public pages.
column6: CK uses SSO and a customer forgot their password. (D) Changing the Salesforce CDN does not affect SAML settings — CDN and SSO/SAML are independent configurations. With SSO enabled, users must reset their password through the Identity Provider (IdP), not through Salesforce's standard password reset flow, as Salesforce delegates authentication to the IdP.

column3: DreamHouse Realty (DR) is looking to enter the insurance business. After discussing with business advisor.DR has decided to use independent agents to manage claims.Which two feature are available for DR to implement a solution involving insurance agent persona?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Financial Services Lightning template | B. Financial Services Community permission setlicense | C. Insurance Agent Portal Lightning template | D. Insurance agent permission set license
column6: DreamHouse Realty entering insurance business with independent agents: (B) Financial Services Community permission set license — provides insurance-specific compliance features. (C) Insurance-specific template or Financial Services template — provides the specific UX and data model components for insurance agents managing claims and policies in an Experience Cloud partner portal.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is building a new self-service site for its large global customer base. Customers will be posting questions, viewing Knowledge articles, downloading warranties, opening tickets, and registering their recent purchases. Purchases are stored in a custom object. UChas decided to use Customer Service template for the experience and Customer Community Plus license for the customers.Which limitation could cause a potential issue for UC?
column4: A. Customer Service template does not allow downloading documents out-of-the-box. | B. Customer Service template does not allow record pages for custom objects out-of-the-box. | C. Customer Community Plus license does not allow access to custom objects. | D. Customer Community Plus license allows only up to 2 million users per org.
column6: UC building a global self-service site for customers needing Knowledge articles. (D) Customer Service template does not allow downloading documents out-of-the-box — this is a relevant capability consideration when choosing a template. Understanding template limitations helps consultants make correct template selections and identify when customization is needed.

column3: Get Cloudy Consulting has decidedto set up and create an Experience Cloud site where customers can create service tickets or chat live with agents.What is the first step the system administrator should take to create the site?
column4: A. Update organization-wide settings. | B. Enable Search EngineOptimization (SEO). | C. Enable Digital Experiences. | D. Configure the default login.
column6: Get Cloudy Consulting setting up an Experience Cloud site. (C) Enable Digital Experiences — before creating any Experience Cloud site, the Salesforce administrator must first enable 'Digital Experiences' (formerly 'Communities') in Setup under Digital Experiences settings. This org-level setting is the prerequisite that unlocks all Experience Cloud functionality.

column3: Universal Containers is looking to onboard three new partners to the community.* Each partner have a branded experience containing their colors and logo.* Gold and silver partners should have access to the Leads inbox component, but Bronze partners should not.* Bronze partners should not have access to the Leads tab.How should an administrator solve for these requirements?
column4: A. Create branding sets, audience targeting, and navigation menu targeting. | B. Create branding sets,audience targeting and a custom Navigation menu component. | C. Create a separate community for each partner with audience targeting. | D. Create branding sets, a separate page variation for each partner, and a custom Navigation Menu component.
column6: UC onboarding three partners with different branding, gold/silver needing lead inbox, bronze not seeing leads tab. (A) Create branding sets (per-partner color/logo), audience targeting (show/hide lead inbox component based on partner tier), and navigation menu targeting (control tab visibility for bronze vs. gold/silver). This three-pronged approach addresses all requirements: branding, component visibility, and navigation.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) wants to create a public site to recruit potential volunteers. Volunteering events are stored in a custom VolunteeringEvent object.How can CK give guest users access to a custom object?
column4: A. Through guest user roles | B. Through guest user Sharing Sets | C. Through guest user organization-wide defaults (OWD) | D. Through guest userSharing Rules
column6: CK wants a public site where guest users can register as volunteers. (C) Through guest user organization-wide defaults — to allow guest users to create records, configure guest user OWD settings and grant the guest user profile Create permission on the custom object. Guest user OWDs and sharing rules are the correct mechanisms for guest record creation.

column3: Get Cloud Consultant (GCC) is implementing a Salesforce- based solution for a global coffee brand. The coffee company works with agrp research and coffee growers from around the world. These researchers will submit their recommendation in the system which will go through an approval process before reaching coffee growers who will ultimately use those recommendation during cultivation.The Design team estimates the need for at least 20 custom objects given that the coffee company plans to use Salesforce to also manage incentives. Compensations, distribution, and projections.Which user license Should GCC recommend for the researchers?
column4: A. Customer Community | B. External Apps | C. Partner Community Plus | D. Customer Community Plus
column6: GCC implementing a Salesforce-based solution for a global organization needing extensive external user access. (B) External Apps license — formerly Customer Community Plus, provides the broadest access for external users with role hierarchy support, sharing rules, and standard/custom object access. Suitable for complex global implementations requiring more than basic community functionality.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) is planning to build a social intranet site as well as an HR help site for its employees using Experience Cloud. Most employees either work in sales or service and currently use Salesforce.Which user license should be recommended for CK's employees to access Experience Cloud sites?
column4: A. Salesforce Authenticated Site | B. Salesforce Unlimited | C. Customer Community | D. Platform Portal
column6: CK wants to build both a social intranet AND an HR help desk. (B) Salesforce Unlimited Edition — creating multiple Experience Cloud sites with advanced employee-facing features requires sufficient edition and license capacity. Unlimited Edition supports multiple Experience Cloud sites and the employee-focused features needed for both an intranet and HR help desk simultaneously.

column3: Ursa Major Solar wants to give customers the ability to add authorized users to view usage, billing, and payment history.Which permission should be granted to customersto add authorized users?
column4: A. Delegated External User Administrator | B. View and Manage Users | C. Modify All for Usage, Billing, and Payment History | D. View Content in Portals
column6: UMS wants to give customers the ability to add authorized sub-users to their account. (A) Delegated External User Administrator permission — by granting this permission to a customer user, they can create and manage other external users under their account. This is the standard Salesforce mechanism for account-level user self-management in Experience Cloud.

column3: Ursa Major Solar (UMS) wants to differentiate between the leads created by partners and the leads created by its ownSales team. All lead records are shared with partners: however, some of the information captured by the Sales team on the lead record should not be shared with partners.Which two steps should UMS take at a minimum in order to meet the requirement?Choose2 answers
column4: A. Create Lead Record Types for Lead Distribution. | B. Enable "Lead Sharing" in Digital Experience settings. | C. Create a separate channel for partners in PRM Workspaces. | D. Create Page Layouts for Lead Distribution.
column6: UMS wants to differentiate partner-created vs. self-generated leads. (A) Create Lead Record Types for Lead Distribution — partner-distributed leads can use a specific Record Type identifying them as partner-originated. (D) Enable 'Lead Sharing' in Digital Experience settings or use custom fields — these mechanisms allow filtering, reporting, and routing leads based on their origin.

column3: Dreamscape flowers is looking to launch a public site for its current customers and prospects.Which three actions are performed automatically when a site is created Experience Cloud?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Creation of a guest role | B. Creation of moderation criteria that are applied only to guest users | C. Creation of a guest profile | D. Creation of sharing mechanisms that are available only to guest users | E. Creation of a guest user record
column6: Dreamscape Flowers launching a public site. Three considerations: (A) Creation of a guest role — automatically created when site is activated. (C) Guest users can only access records via Guest User Sharing Rules, not role hierarchy. (E) Guest users accessing the site are counted in monthly page view limits even without logging in. These are specific guest user considerations for public Experience Cloud sites.

column3: Which three topic types can be used in an Aura site?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Content Topic | B. Standard Topic | C. Featured Topic | D. Navigational Topic | E. Deleted Topic
column6: Three topic types in an Aura Experience Cloud site: (B) Standard Topics — user-created topics for general content organization. (C) Featured Topics — curated prominent topics appearing in the Featured Topics component on the home page. (D) Navigational Topics — hierarchically organized topics serving as site navigation structure. 'Content Topic' (A) and 'Deleted Topic' (E) are not valid topic type names.

column3: Universal Containers is implementing a customer community.What sharing mechanism should be used to allow customers to view their own cases even after those cases are assigned to a support agent?
column4: A. OWD and Apex Sharing | B. Sharing Set | C. Case co-ownership using Super User access | D. Sharing Map and custom permission set
column6: Universal Containers implementing a customer community. (B) Sharing Set — the primary mechanism for Customer Community license users to access records related to their account or contact. A Sharing Set grants access to records where a lookup field on the record matches the user's account or contact. This is the standard sharing tool for Customer Community users.

column3: A manager at Ursa Major Solar is responsible for creating and editing only the community users for the Partner Community they have recently set up using Experience Cloud. However, the manager is able to make organizational changes to the user records of the internal users as web.What could be the possibleissue here?
column4: A. The manager has been given the Manage Profiles and Permission Sets permission. | B. The manager has been given the Manage Customer Users permission. | C. The manager has been given the Manage User permission. | D. The manager has been given the Manage External Users permission.
column6: A manager at UMS needs to create and edit ONLY community users (not all Salesforce users). (C) The manager has been given Delegated External User Administration privileges — this role-specific permission allows the manager to create and edit external community users without System Administrator access to the full Salesforce org.

column3: A consultant needs to leverage ExperienceBundle for a deployment but isunable to view it.What is the most likely cause for this issue?
column4: A. The experience has not yet been published. | B. A change set containing the Network needs to be deployed. | C. The "Enable ExperienceBundle Metadata API" setting needs to be checked. | D. A custom Experience template needs to be created.
column6: A consultant needs ExperienceBundle for deployment but cannot see it. (C) A change set containing the Network needs to be deployed — ExperienceBundle is only retrievable after the site has been created and activated. The Network metadata (which creates the site) must be deployed first. The site must exist before the ExperienceBundle can be retrieved.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters has configured chat so customers can quickly get answers to their questions bychatting with an agent while browsing the Experience site.How should an administrator embed the chat window in an Experience site?
column4: A. Add the Embedded Service component to Builder pages. | B. Create a custom component using the Embedded Service API. | C. Configure the Embedded Service connector. | D. Create an Embedded Service quick action.
column6: NTO configured chat for customers on their Experience site. (A) Add the Embedded Service component to Builder pages — the Embedded Service component (from Setup > Embedded Service Deployments) is placed on site pages to provide the chat/messaging interface. This is the standard method for adding chat or messaging to Experience Cloud pages.

column3: Whatare three goals Ursa Major Solar can accomplish with experience Cloud moderation functionality?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Allow members to remove other member from the Experience site if desired. | B. Track Flagging and moderation activity within the Experience site. | C. Allow members to flag posts comments files, and messages that are inappropriate or spam. | D. Designate specific users as moderators so that they can closely monitor the size. | E. Give members Audience Targeting permissions within the Experience site.
column6: Three goals UMS can accomplish with Experience Cloud moderation: (B) Track Flagging and flag content for review — content flagging workflows are part of moderation. (C) Set up automated moderation rules — rules can auto-remove or hide content based on keywords or patterns. (D) Control member behavior (freeze/block abusive users) — moderation enables suspending or banning disruptive members. Allowing members to remove others (A) is not standard.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) is planning to introduce a User Acceptance Testing (UAT) process to ensure quality. UAT will take place In Partial and Full sandboxes. OC has also set up the Salesforce content Delivery Network (CDN) for its domain in production environment.What should CK keep in mind about salesforce CDN?
column4: A. Salesforce CDN is only supported in Full sandbox environments. | B. Salesforce CDN is not supported in sandbox environments. | C. Salesforce CDN is only supported in Developer sandbox environments | D. Salesforce CDN is supported in all sandbox environment
column6: CK is planning UAT for their Experience Cloud site. (B) Salesforce CDN is not supported in sandbox environments — this is a critical fact for UAT planning. CDN performance testing cannot be done in sandbox, so UAT teams must be aware that CDN behavior observed in production will differ from sandbox testing. This affects performance benchmarking during UAT.

column3: By defining roles, permission sets, or profiles, Knowledge article visibility can be controlled by using which functionality?
column4: A. Data Category Visibility | B. Content Management | C. Automatic Topic Assignment | D. Org-Wide Defaults
column6: Knowledge article visibility can be controlled by roles, permission sets, or profiles. (A) Data Category Visibility — in Salesforce, data category visibility settings determine which users see articles in specific categories. Visibility is assigned based on user roles, permission sets, or profiles, giving flexible control over which user groups see which category of Knowledge articles.

column3: Ursa Major Solar (UMS) has a discussion thread on its Experience Cloud site for customers to provide feedback on the company's latest product release. To help ensure new members understand what the discussion thread is meant for and to monitor their first posts, UMS would like to approve the first post of any new member who has joined within 7 days or less.What is needed to configure this?
column4: A. Create a moderation rule with the appropriate content criteria. | B. Create a Community moderator with Delete permissions. | C. Create a Community moderator with Approval permissions. | D. Create a moderation rule with the appropriate member criteria.
column6: UMS has a discussion thread on its Experience Cloud site for a specific product issue. (D) Create a Community moderator — for managing discussion threads, designating a community moderator allows specific users to pin, edit, delete, and manage discussion posts. Moderators can ensure product-related discussions remain on-topic and appropriately managed.

column3: Ursa Major Solar (UMS) would like to render a header and footer from an external content management system into its customer portal.Which feature should UMS use to accomplish this?
column4: A. Developer Console | B. Compact Header Properties | C. Rich Content Editor | D. CMS Connect
column6: UMS wants to render a header and footer from an external website. (D) CMS Connect — allows Experience Cloud sites to pull content from external CMS systems via REST API or JSON. CMS Connect is specifically designed to integrate external CMS content (including headers, footers, navigation) into Experience Cloud pages, making it the correct tool for this use case.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) updates its Salesforce CMS content often to meet the needs of its customers. The site builder wants to use a template with out-of-the-box components that dynamically update the CMS content on its Experience Cloud site.Which template should the site builder use?
column4: A. Build Your Own (Aura) | B. Customer Service | C. Salesforce Tabs + Visualforce | D. Build Your Own (LWR)
column6: UC frequently updates Salesforce CMS content and needs to choose the right template. (D) Build Your Own (LWR) — LWR-based sites have better CMS integration and support real-time CMS content updates more efficiently than Aura-based templates. Build Your Own (LWR) is the recommended modern template for CMS-heavy sites requiring frequent content updates.

column3: The system administrator at Cloud Kicks (CK) has deactivated their Experience Cloud site to do some maintenance and cleanup.How should the administrator ensure that CK custorners do NOTreceive a welcome email when the site is once again active?
column4: A. Use the new Service Not Available (SNA) feature. | B. Use Data Loader to remove all members' email addresses. | C. Disable the Send welcome email checkbox for the site. | D. Remove all profiles fromthe site's membership and add them again after the site is activated.
column6: CK system administrator deactivated their Experience Cloud site for maintenance. (C) Use the new Service Not Available (SNA) feature — when a site is deactivated, Experience Cloud redirects members to a maintenance/service unavailable page. Administrators can configure a custom maintenance message or page to communicate site unavailability to visitors during the maintenance window.

column3: Ursa Major Solar (UMS) has business and person accounts in its Salesforce org. UMS has partner portals created for its Silver partners, DreamHouse Realty (DR) and Cloud Kicks (CK).UMS's Experience team is creating users for its partners. DR and CK users do not require access to opportunities, leads, and campaigns.What are the two considerations for creating partner users and granting access?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Only business accounts can be created as partner users | B. Assign Partner Community license to partner users. | C. Assign Customer Community Plus license to partner users. | D. Only person accounts can be created as partner users.
column6: UMS has both business and person accounts and wants to use Experience Cloud: (B) Assign Partner Community license to partner accounts and create community users — TRUE for partner users associated with business accounts. (C) Person accounts can also be enabled as Experience Cloud users — TRUE, person account contacts can be enabled as community users. Both account types can coexist as community members.

column3: Ursa Major Solar (UMS) has seen exponential growth in recent years. The Incoming call volume to the support center has gone up exponentially as well, and is now reaching unmanageable levels.What should UMS consider to help the situation?
column4: A. Create a peer-to-peer forum using Self Service template. | B. Create a self service community using Customer Service template. | C. Create a smart queue router using Service Cloud template. | D. Create virtual support agents using Chat Bot template.
column6: UMS has exponential support volume growth. (B) Create a self-service community using Customer Service template — a self-service Experience Cloud site with Knowledge base, FAQ, and peer-to-peer Q&A enables customers to find answers independently. This deflects a significant percentage of inbound support contacts before they reach agents.

column3: Cloud Kicks has noticed its new Experience Cloud site is taking a long time to load.Which tool should the system administrator use to troubleshoot the problem?
column4: A. Salesforce Community Page Optimizer | B. Lightning Page Maximizer | C. Experience Cloud Performance Profiler | D. Cloud Network Optimization Bolt
column6: Cloud Kicks noticed their new Experience Cloud site is loading slowly. (A) Salesforce Community Page Optimizer — available in Experience Builder, this tool analyzes individual pages and identifies performance bottlenecks (too many components, large images, inefficient configurations). It provides specific recommendations for improving page load times.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK)wants to organize content on its site so that users can easily search and brows for information.Which three features should CK use to accomplish this goal?
column4: A. Navigational Topics | B. Content Topics | C. Content Graph | D. Featured Topic | E. Navigation Tree
column6: CK wants to organize content for easy search and navigation. Three applicable solutions: (B) Content Topics — allow tagging content with topics for discovery. (C) Content Graph — surfaces related content based on topic relationships. (E) Navigation Trails (breadcrumbs) — help users understand and navigate the content hierarchy. These three work together to create an organized, navigable content experience.

column3: What are two ways a question can be escalated to a case?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Manually by a moderator selecting ''Escalate to Case'' in the Feed | B. Manually by users commenting ''Escalate'' | C. Automatically via Case Assignment Rules | D. Automatically via process Builder by meeting specified criteria
column6: Two ways a question can be escalated to a case: (A) Manually by a moderator selecting 'Escalate to Case' in the Feed — TRUE, moderators can manually escalate any question post to a case from the community feed. (D) Automatically through moderation rules or question-to-case automation — TRUE. These two paths (manual moderator action and automated rules) are the standard escalation mechanisms.

column3: A consultant for Cloud Kicks (CK) is asked to build a site for CK customers. As part of this site, a custom object will be used to manage customer subscriptions. These subscriptions will need to leverage advanced sharing rules to ensure that only appropriate customers can see these subscriptions.Which two user license types should be granted to customers to support this sharingrequirement?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Partner Community User | B. Customer Community Login User | C. Customer Community User | D. Customer Community Plus Login User
column6: A consultant building a site for CK customers needs to select appropriate license types. (A) Partner Community User — for partner/retailer-facing portals needing deal registration and lead management. (D) Customer Community Plus User — for customers needing access to records with role hierarchy support. The specific use case determines the appropriate license for each user type.

column3: Bloomington Caregivers (BC) intends to launch a company-wide project to create personalized experiences for its providers, vendors installers, and patients. BC's business processes and workflow flow industry standards and common practices, mostly driven by compliance and regulatory mandates.What should BC closely into during the evaluation phase?
column4: A. Lightning Bolt solutions | B. Community Connect | C. Digital Experience framework | D. SDLC (Software Developer Life Cycle) for Experiences
column6: Bloomington Caregivers launching a company-wide Experience Cloud project. (A) Lightning Bolt solutions — pre-built, packaged Experience Cloud sites available on AppExchange. They include templates, data models, and configurations for specific industries/use cases (healthcare, financial services), enabling rapid deployment for large company-wide projects.

column3: Zephyrus Relocation Services (ZRS) plans to build a portal for its partners. The portal needs to show company information and brand details on the Account Management page.Which templates shouldZRS consider to build the portal?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Partner Central | B. Build Your Own | C. Help Center | D. Customer service
column6: ZRS building a partner portal with account management and lead sharing: (A) Partner Central — purpose-built Salesforce template for partner portals with lead management, deal registration, and collaboration. (B) Build Your Own — for highly customized partner experiences. Partner Central is the primary recommendation; Build Your Own provides flexibility for unique requirements.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK)is about to launch a public site and is expecting very high traffic in certain regions. CK will be using Content Delivery Network (CDN).What should CK consider during the go-live phase to prevent usability issues?
column4: A. CK should provision and activate CDN in those regions where traffic is high. | B. CK should provision CDN in those regions where traffic is high and activate CDN where traffic is low. | C. CK should provision and activate CDN in those regions where traffic is low | D. CK should provision CDN in those regions where traffic is low and activate CDN where traffic is high.
column6: CK launching a public site expecting very high traffic. (A) Provision and activate CDN in regions where traffic is high — Salesforce CDN caches guest user pages at edge servers geographically close to visitors, reducing page load times and Salesforce server load. This is the most effective approach for handling high-volume public site traffic.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitter is looking to implement a public Knowledge base. The company has 1, 000 articles stored in an external systems.Some of the articles are more than 2 years old.What should a consultant recommend to optimize the public knowledge base?
column4: A. Manually add the top performing articles to Salesforce and select Authenticated Sites as the Channel. | B. Bulk-import all articles to Salesforce and archive duplicate and outdated articles. | C. Manually add the top performing articles to Salesforce and select the Public Knowledge Base channel. | D. Bulk-import the top performing articles to Salesforce and select the public knowledge base channel.
column6: NTO wants a public Knowledge base with 1,000 articles, some outdated. (D) Use Knowledge article archiving/expiration dates — when importing or managing articles, administrators set expiration dates on outdated content. Articles past their expiration date are automatically archived and removed from public view, keeping the knowledge base current without manual maintenance.

column3: Northern Trail outfitters (NTO) aims to provide personalization by encouraging its individual customers to self-register in its B2C Experience site. NTO is not looking to create a placeholder account.NTO Experience consultant has set up self-registration in its Login and Registration pages.
